fields.summary: Limit queue creation depth relative to its first static parent
[Umbrella] Support Flexible Auto Queue Creation in Capacity Scheduler

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description: YARN-10506 introduced a limit on the maximum depth of auto queue creation. This, however, only limits the levels of queue path relative to its first existing parent queue. It poses an unnecessary limitation on users, while providing no real safety net over rogue users (especially when YARN-10632 makes this limit configurable), because it could be incrementally circumvented by creating a new queue under an existing dynamic parent queue. By bounding this limit to the first static parent queue in the hierarchy, we could have a safer alternative.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Make auto queue creation maximum allowed depth configurable

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

comments.body: Thanks [~gandras] for this, i will review this, and adjust YARN-10632 when this is finished.:D

Uploaded the first revision of this issue. I have chosen to check for the first static parent in two cycles (which could be merged to one while loop, but would hurt the readability a lot in my opinion). This, however involves the same amount of iteration as if it was a single pass check.

Thank you [~pbacsko] for the review. I have thought about this approach, but the first createQueue could also throw such exceptions, therefore I have limited the expected exception block to the second queue creation.
Thanks [~gandras] for patch, LGTM +1, and i will rebase YARN-10632 based this.:D

Thanks [~gandras] for the patch and [~zhuqi] for the review.

Committed to trunk.

Thank you [~pbacsko] for the review. I have thought about this approach, but the first createQueue could also throw such exceptions, therefore I have limited the expected exception block to the second queue creation.
Thanks [~gandras] for patch, LGTM +1, and i will rebase YARN-10632 based this.:D

Thanks [~gandras] for the patch and [~zhuqi] for the review.

Committed to trunk.
